title: Broad home-directory filesystem access is a hard blocker

origin.description: Contract seed: a filesystem server granted access to /Users/example (a home root) instead of the workspace. Replace with a redacted real-world snapshot before R2 final acceptance.

humanExpectation.rationale: Granting an agent-invocable tool access to an entire home directory exposes credentials, SSH keys, and unrelated private files; CallLint treats broad local filesystem access as a critical blocker, not a soft review.
knownLimitations: A developer may intentionally grant broad access for a local-only experiment; the false-positive note records this.
